methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $205M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Strand Therapeutics is a Series B-stage biotech company founded by Jake Becraft and Tasuku Kitada. The firm has raised $205M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 8 private biotech firms at the same stage and 3 public-market peers in the Biotech & Health sector.

Two quantitative methods are applicable. Stage-Progression analysis applies a typical 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $205M total capital raised, yielding an implied valuation of $390M. Peer-Median anchoring uses five same-sector, same-stage private comparables—Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M)—to derive a median peer valuation of $810M. This peer set spans $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied; the sector P/S multiple for Biotech & Health stands at 7.8x across three public tickers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L), but Strand's revenue is not disclosed.

The triangulated range is $331M–$931M, with a median of $600M. This reflects material divergence between the two applicable methods: stage-progression suggests a more conservative $390M floor, while peer benchmarking suggests higher multiples are sustained in this market. Key uncertainty centers on Strand's clinical-stage progress and intellectual property defensibility relative to the peer set, which spans a wide valuation spread.
